name: Bali
description: Bali, also known as Mahabali, was a benevolent Asura king celebrated for his wisdom, generosity, and unwavering devotion to his people. Born into the Asura clan, Bali inherited a kingdom marked by prosperity and righteousness. His reign was characterized by equality, justice, and the well-being of all his subjects, earning him immense popularity and respect throughout the three worlds. He expanded his kingdom through righteous means, never resorting to deceit or unjust warfare. Bali's power and influence grew so vast that even the gods felt threatened, fearing he might usurp their position. Despite being an Asura, Bali embodied Dharma and upheld moral principles, making him a complex and compelling figure in Hindu mythology. His story serves as a reminder that righteousness transcends birth and lineage. Ultimately, his devotion to Vishnu led to his downfall at the hands of Vamana, an avatar of Vishnu. However, impressed by Bali's unwavering commitment to his promise, Vishnu granted him the boon to rule the netherworld (Patala) and visit his kingdom on Earth once a year during Onam, a festival celebrated in Kerala, India. This annual visit symbolizes Bali's continued care for his people and his enduring legacy as a just and beloved ruler. His tale emphasizes the importance of Dharma, devotion, and the cyclical nature of power.
subtitle: Benevolent Asura King of Prosperity
achievements: Righteous rule over the three worlds
Generosity and compassion towards his subjects
Defeat of Indra and expansion of his kingdom
Boons granted by Vishnu for his devotion

keyMyths.title: The Vamana Avatar
keyMyths.description: Vishnu incarnates as Vamana, a dwarf brahmin, and approaches Bali during a Yajna. Vamana requests three paces of land, which Bali grants, unaware of Vamana's true nature.

keyMyths.title: Bali's Downfall
keyMyths.description: Vamana grows to immense size and covers the earth and heavens in two paces.  Unable to fulfill the third pace, Bali offers his own head, demonstrating his commitment to his promise.

keyMyths.title: Granting of Patala
keyMyths.description: Impressed by Bali's righteousness and devotion, Vishnu grants him the rule over Patala (the netherworld) and allows him to visit his kingdom on Earth once a year during Onam.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: Contains the most detailed account of Bali's life and interactions with Vamana.
sources.text: Contains the most detailed account of Bali's life and interactions with Vamana.

sources.author: Various Authors
sources.description: Different Puranas contain variations of the Bali and Vamana story.
sources.text: Different Puranas contain variations of the Bali and Vamana story.
